title: Principal Engineer, Supplier Development
description:
Step into a career with ASM, where cutting edge technology meets collaborative culture. ​

For over 55 years ASM has been ahead of what’s next, at the forefront of innovation and what’s technologically possible. With more than 4,500 ASMers representing 70 nationalities, our people and our advanced semiconductor devices are playing a crucial role in trends such as 5G, cloud computing, AI, and autonomous driving.  But we’re more than just a tech company. We value diversity, inclusion and sustainability as we strive to make a positive impact on the world.  Our development programs help support your growth, shaping your future and pushing the boundaries of innovation to unleash potential.  

Job's mission

As a key member of our Global Supplier Development team, this role supports the organization's objective to strengthen supplier capability, elevate design and manufacturing performance, and ensure long-term supply resilience across our global network. The mission of the team is to proactively build supplier maturity, drive continuous improvement, and align supplier capability growth with mid- and long-term category strategies to support product quality, operational excellence, and New Product Introduction (NPI) readiness.

What you will be working on

  • Assess supplier quality and technology gaps, and execute plans to address them.
  • Review and influence Design Engineering early in design phase to achieve target costs and DFx. Identify areas for cost down during design phase plus manufacturing methods and eliminate waste.
  • Evaluate supplier processes and risks; collaborate to improve capabilities through lean supplier development methods (i.e. six sigma) and implement mitigation plans.
  • Apply engineering and material science expertise with suppliers to boost capacity, product quality, yield and reduce costs.
  • Partner with Category leaders to drive long-term category strategies to support aggressive production scaling.
  • Align development efforts with long-term category strategies and develop partners within the suppliers.
  • Conduct on-site supplier assessments (capabilities, MSA, PCS/SPCE, etc) and identify gaps during selection.
  • Implement improvement plans with suppliers to ensure readiness and support NPI.
  • Coordinate globally with Business/Key Product Unit Engineering teams to meet end-use specifications.
  • Advance manufacturing capabilities, DFMEA / FMEA, and implement Process Control Systems, PFMEA, at key sites.
  • Support statistical PCS / SPC (lean six sigma) data analysis for internal and external stakeholders.
  • Perform root cause failure analysis and report findings to relevant teams.
  • Lead supplier engagement, support surveillance audits, and qualification activities globally.
  • Independently manage supplier improvement projects, including onboarding training and qualification.
  • Oversee multiple projects to resolve issues and enhance product performance, reliability, and efficiency.
  • Lead / mentor group of Supplier Development team on best practices and develop skillsets to support group growth and expansion

What we are looking for

  • MS, or PhD in Engineering, Science, or related technical field (Engineering, Physics, Material Sciences) relevant to semiconductor equipment, or proven expertise.
  • At least 10 years of experience or significant expertise in engineering, designing, and manufacturing of complex mechanical systems.
  • Experience in the semiconductor capital equipment industry with technical background in semiconductor manufacturing technologies required.
  • Strong English communication skills for complex technical topics.
  • Effective collaboration across regions, cultures, and disciplines.
  • Proven Lead Engineer in matrix environments and ability to work independently while managing a team.
  • Proficient in project and time management skills required
  • Drive business process efficiencies and improvements with key internal stakeholders

Candidate should have knowledge of the following tools, but will be trained to more advanced levels in:

  • Applied Operational and Engineering Statistical Methods
  • Process Control Systems (PCS) and Statistical Process Control (SPC)
  • FMEA include Design FMEA (DFMEA) and Process FMEA (PFMEA)
  • Structured Problem Solving (8D) and Effective Problem Solving (EPS)
  • Operational Excellence tools including Failure Analysis, Lean Manufacturing, Value Stream Mapping
  • Project Management, Team Management, Escalation Management and Communication Techniques across a broad range of business escalation types, (Customer, Supplier, Technical, Manufacturing, etc.)
  • Should costing estimate modeling (aPriori)

Apply today to be part of what’s next.

We make the tech that enables the chips in devices which improve lives around the world. We do this with an eye to the future, pushing the boundaries of what’s possible through cutting-edge innovation, and driving the next wave of technological breakthroughs that shape how we live, work, and connect.

To learn more about ASM, find us at asm.com and on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, X and YouTube.

ASM is an equal opportunity employer and considers qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, nationality, social or ethnic origin, sexual orientation, gender, gender identify or expression, marital status, pregnancy, political affiliation, disability, genetic information, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

postedCompany.description:
ASM is a leading global supplier of products, services, and materials for semiconductor processing. For more than half a century, innovation has been at the core of everything we do. Our smart, ambitious people are dedicated to creating cutting-edge solutions for the world’s leading semiconductor providers. Every day we push the development of next-generation computer-chip technology, always staying a few steps ahead of what’s next.

The talented, enthusiastic people at ASM are just like you: dedicated to improving people’s lives and unlocking new potential. With our collaborative approach to R&D, we advance key semiconductor technologies and platforms like ALD, epitaxy, PEALD, PECVD, and vertical furnaces. And we have recently entered the promising high-growth market: silicon carbide epitaxy. Year after year, our innovations help to make chips smaller, faster, and more powerful. In fact, you will find our technology in every aspect of modern life: in AI, medical equipment, 5G, smartphones, autonomous driving, and more.


Be part of our exciting future and apply today! careers.asm.com/global/en/
postedCompany.name: ASM FRONT-END MANUFACTURING SINGAPORE PTE. LTD.
